title: IMPACT by Amir Shpilman
summary: Engraving and computer-assisted orchestration for a new opera. Developed a custom Sibelius plugin for real-time material orchestration.

contentBlocks.value: **IMPACT by Amir Shpilman** is an ambitious contemporary opera for orchestra, choir, and two stuntwomen. The score demanded versatile and advanced notation techniques. Our role focused on engraving, but the scope and complexity required the development of custom digital solutions under intense production pressure. These methods not only streamlined the engraving process but also enabled faster creation of performance materials, optimized rehearsal time, and improved clarity in interpreting the score through **computer-assisted engraving techniques**.

contentBlocks.alt: Scene from IMPACT opera showing complex staging

contentBlocks.value: A central technical challenge was the rapid generation and orchestration of musical material. To solve this, we developed a **custom Sibelius plugin** tailored to real-time material transformation. It allowed systematic orchestration and notation aligned directly with the composer's workflow. The plugin enabled quick iteration based on rehearsal feedback and supported precise control over the score's visual and musical complexity. Its integration was crucial in managing the interplay between music and tightly synchronized stage action, including the stunt choreography.

contentBlocks.alt: Close-up of the custom Sibelius plugin interface
contentBlocks.caption: The plugin written with ManuScript™️ for Sibelius®

contentBlocks.value: Further information about the performance and its reception can be found on the [official project page](https://www.cultopia.gr/projects/Impact).

title: Kriat Shema by Amir Shpilman (2025)
summary: Score, parts, and performance materials for Amir Shpilman's 'Kriat Shema,' a Fantasy for Shofar and Orchestra. Premiered by the **Berlin Philharmonic**.

contentBlocks.value:
**ZSCORE.art studio**, led by **Parham Behzad**, was responsible for the preparation of the full score, individual parts, and all performance materials for Amir Shpilman's *Kriat Shema – Fantasy for Shofar and Orchestra*.

This was our second collaboration with the composer. The work was premiered by the **Berlin Philharmonic** and is dedicated to **Bar Zemach** and **Yehezkel Tchorz** (RIP).

contentBlocks.alt: Detail from the score of Kriat Shema

contentBlocks.value:
The engraving and preparation process emphasized clarity in the orchestral textures and supported the integration of the Shofar—a non-standard orchestral instrument requiring careful handling in notation.

All rights to the work are held by **Amir Shpilman**. Score typesetting and engraving by **Parham Behzad (ZSCORE.art)**.

title: Engraving for Mass Choir Collaborative Performance
summary: Current, large-scale engraving project for a collaborative performance piece involving a mass choir of over 100 singers.

contentBlocks.value:
This ongoing project involves the detailed engraving of a new work designed for a **mass choir with more than 100 singers**. The focus is on creating clear, accurate, and performer-friendly scores and parts to facilitate a large-scale collaborative performance.

The nature of the work demands careful attention to layout and part extraction to manage the complexities of a large ensemble.

contentBlocks.alt: Example of choral engraving style for the project
contentBlocks.caption: Score preparation for over 100 singers
